_comment: Small LLM kept resident alongside ComfyUI while it's actively generating -- activated automatically the moment a POST /comfyui/prompt request comes through the proxy (see handle_comfyui_proxy/activate_comfy_coexist in llama_priority_proxy.py), never manually needed. comfy_reserved_vram_bytes is subtracted from this scenario's own budget calc (on top of the usual headroom_gb and standalone VRAM) to leave room for whatever checkpoint is loaded in ComfyUI -- size it to your largest expected checkpoint. fallback_for covers the scenarios that should keep answering chat requests, served by this same resident model, while this is active (see find_fallback) -- add more names here if more day-to-day scenarios exist later. IMPORTANT: this scenario's own 'priority' must stay HIGHER than every scenario named in fallback_for, or handle_completion's outranks_active check skips find_fallback entirely (going straight to a real, disruptive switch) instead of ever trying the fallback -- bump this if a future day-to-day scenario is added above priority 100. 'eager': true forces this member to spawn the instant the scenario activates (see eager_load_pinned_members) rather than waiting for a chat request to trigger the normal on-demand spawn -- without it, find_fallback would find nothing resident yet and every concurrent chat request would force a real (disruptive) scenario switch instead of the intended fallback. Deliberately NOT done via the model's global keep_alive=-1, since qwen2.5-coder-1.5b should stay lazy in its other (coding) scenario appearance. No --mmproj here, so a vision request (or anything else not covered by fallback_for) isn't silently misrouted -- but it also does NOT force a real scenario switch like an ordinary fallback-miss would: ComfyUI holds its comfy_reserved_vram_bytes chunk for the ENTIRE time this scenario is active (freed only when watch_comfy_queue_and_revert actually reverts), so evicting this model wouldn't free enough room for anything else to load anyway. handle_completion instead returns a graceful 503 (code image_generation_busy) and leaves this scenario running until the queue drains on its own.
name: comfy_coexist
